subject: Careers
topic: Paper airplane prototype progression and improvement checklist

teacher_guide.title: Careers 8 Teacher Guide - Paper Airplane Prototype Challenge
teacher_guide.overview: Two-day prototype challenge. Students design, test, improve, and reflect on a paper airplane prototype. The focus is evidence-based improvement, teamwork, perseverance, communication, safety, and career skill reflection.
teacher_guide.essential_question: How do people improve a product or process by using evidence, feedback, and perseverance?
teacher_guide.opening_frame: Today you are working like a small product design team. Good products usually improve through prototypes, testing, feedback, and revision.

timing.activity: Launch challenge, safety, and the rule: one change, one test, one conclusion.
Prototype 0 baseline build and first test.
Prototype 1 stability change and evidence recording.
Prototype 2 distance or accuracy change and evidence recording.
Exit ticket: best prototype, evidence, and Tuesday improvement plan.
Final build, tune, and quick test using Monday evidence.
Official flights: two launches per group.
Final reflection and career skill connection.

teacher_notes: Do not assess only the farthest flight. Assess evidence, improvement, safe process, and explanation.
Stop random rebuilding by asking: What changed from the last version?
Use the Monday exit ticket as the bridge into Tuesday work time.
Keep launch line, retrieval routine, and no-throwing-at-people rule explicit.
teacher_guide.project_prompt: Design, test, improve, and officially fly a paper airplane prototype. Teams must show prototype progression, evidence-based changes, and a final reflection connecting the work to a career skill.
matching_bank: Twists, dives, or flips: check wing symmetry, nose tightness, wing tips, and balance.
Straight but short flight: try sharper folds, narrower wings, one small weight, or consistent force.
Far but off course: check wing angles, bent folds, one wing tip, and launch angle.
Team disagreement: return to data and choose one testable change.

model.title: Teacher model
model.sample: Prototype 1 flew straighter than the baseline because we made the wings more even. The straight-flight score improved from 1 to 2. On Tuesday, we will keep the wing symmetry and test one nose-weight change to improve distance.

teacher_guide.assessment_focus: Assessment focus: prototype progression, evidence-based improvement, safe team process, and a final reflection that connects a career skill to the work.

task_sheet.title: Careers 8 Student Task - Paper Airplane Prototype Challenge
instructions: Monday is build, test, and improve day.
Tuesday begins with final work time, then official flights.
Change one main thing at a time so your team can tell what helped.
Use the tracker, improvement cards, exit ticket, rubric, and reflection.

tasks.label: Part A
tasks.prompt: Set team roles: designer, test pilot, data recorder, and quality checker. Roles can rotate.

tasks.label: Part B
tasks.prompt: Build Prototype 0 as your baseline. Do not overthink this version. It gives your team a starting point.

tasks.label: Part C
tasks.prompt: Build Prototype 1 by changing one feature for stability. Record only the main change.

tasks.label: Part D
tasks.prompt: Build Prototype 2 by changing one feature for distance or accuracy. Use evidence, not guessing.

tasks.label: Part E
tasks.prompt: Tuesday final prototype: use Monday evidence to choose one final improvement before the official launches.

tasks.label: Part F
tasks.prompt: Official result: record two launches and circle your best result.

tasks.label: Part G
tasks.prompt: Completion check: select each item your team can prove before handing in your work.

choice_options: We tested more than one prototype.
We changed one main thing at a time.
We used evidence for Tuesday's final change.
We connected the work to a career skill.

embedded_supports: Sentence frame: Our best prototype was Prototype ___ because ___.
Sentence frame: One change that helped was ___ because ___.
Sentence frame: One career skill I used was ___ because ___.
success_criteria: I built and tested more than one prototype.
I changed only one main thing at a time.
I used evidence to choose a final improvement.
I connected my work to a career skill.

graphic_organizer.title: Prototype Progression Tracker
graphic_organizer.prompt: Use this tracker to show how your design changed from Monday to Tuesday. Record evidence after each test.
success_criteria: I recorded a change and result for each prototype.

station_cards.title: Prototype Improvement Reference Cards
instructions: Use these cards when your team is stuck.
Choose only one main change before testing again.
Record the result before moving to the next prototype.

cards.title: Stability Fix
cards.prompt: Use this if the plane twists, dives, flips, or crashes quickly.
cards.task: Check wing symmetry, nose tightness, wing tips, and balance. Choose one change only.
cards.answer_key: Evidence should name the change and whether flight control improved.
cards.teacher_note: Good card for early failure and regulation.

cards.title: Distance Fix
cards.prompt: Use this if the plane flies straight but does not travel far.
cards.task: Try sharper folds, narrower wings, one small paper clip, or a consistent launch force. Choose one change only.
cards.answer_key: Evidence should compare distance before and after the change.
cards.teacher_note: Keep students from adding too much weight.

cards.title: Accuracy Fix
cards.prompt: Use this if the plane travels far but goes off course.
cards.task: Check even wing angles, bent folds, one wing tip adjustment, or launch angle. Choose one change only.
cards.answer_key: Evidence should describe straighter flight or target improvement.
cards.teacher_note: Good chance to reinforce fair testing.

cards.title: Team Process Fix
cards.prompt: Use this if the group cannot agree on what to do next.
cards.task: Return to your data and choose the most testable next change.
cards.answer_key: Evidence should connect the decision to a prior test result.
cards.teacher_note: Use this to redirect argument into evidence.

rubric_sheet.title: Paper Airplane Prototype Challenge Rubric
rubric_sheet.purpose_line: Assess the design process, not only the farthest flight.

criteria.name: Prototype process
criteria.descriptor: The team built multiple versions and changed one feature at a time.

criteria.name: Evidence and improvement
criteria.descriptor: The team recorded test results and used evidence to make design decisions.

criteria.name: Career skill reflection
criteria.descriptor: The final reflection connects the work to a real career skill such as teamwork, communication, accuracy, safety, patience, or project management.

success_criteria: The rating matches evidence from the tracker.
The final reflection explains the process, not just the result.

exit_ticket.title: Monday Exit Ticket - Prototype Plan
exit_ticket.prompt: Our best prototype today was Prototype ___. The evidence that proves this is ___. One problem we still need to fix is ___. On Tuesday, we will change ___ because ___.
success_criteria: I named the best prototype, used evidence, and planned one Tuesday improvement.
